title: DM-08 â€” Dual-mode docs and config examples
body:
## Context

Document dual mode for users.

## Current state

No dual-mode docs.

## Inputs

Final shapes from DM-01..DM-07.

## Outputs

Updated `README.md`, `USAGE.md`, `config.example.json` (commented dual block), and roadmap.

## Implementation scope

- Add dual-mode quickstart, screenshots/ascii of A/B panes, troubleshooting (per-slot halted, tts_source).

## Acceptance criteria

Docs reflect shipped behaviour; example dual config boots.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- Manual review.
- `config.example.json` parses.

## Dependencies / parallelization

Blocked by DM-01..DM-07. Lands last in cluster.

## Agent routing

Build: docs specialist. Cross-review: tui-rust-code-reviewer.

title: MACOS-01 — Spike: macOS capture API, TCC, CoreAudio/BlackHole, and ScreenCaptureKit decision
body:
## Summary

    Resolve macOS audio-capture confidence gaps before implementation starts.

    ## Why is this needed?

    ScreenCaptureKit bindings, TCC behavior, CoreAudio device lifecycle, BlackHole capture, and latency are below confidence 1.0. Implementation must not begin from guesses.

    ## Context

    The Windows path uses WASAPI loopback behind `src/audio/wasapi_capture.rs`; macOS needs an equivalent plan that preserves the `AudioSource` contract and the 16 kHz mono i16 pipeline.

    ## Inputs

    - `src/audio/mod.rs`
- `src/audio/wasapi_capture.rs`
- `Cargo.toml` target dependencies
- Opus macOS research risk list
- macOS 13/14/15 test hosts or runners

    ## Outputs

    - Decision record in issue comments
- Chosen tier-1 and tier-2 capture strategy
- Rejected alternatives with rationale
- Implementation prerequisites for MACOS-02 and MACOS-03

    ## Test cases

    - [ ] Build a CoreAudio/cpal hello-capture that emits 16 kHz mono i16 chunks
- [ ] Capture from BlackHole 2ch for 60 seconds and measure sample-continuity
- [ ] Prototype ScreenCaptureKit audio-only capture or Objective-C/Swift trampoline
- [ ] Confirm permission-denied/TCC behavior from terminal launch
- [ ] Measure first-sample and steady-state latency on at least one Apple Silicon host

    ## Acceptance criteria

    - [ ] Confidence reaches 1.0 for starting MACOS-02
- [ ] Confidence reaches 1.0 or a follow-up blocker is recorded for MACOS-03
- [ ] Soundflower and bundled-driver paths are explicitly rejected
- [ ] TCC remediation path is documented
- [ ] Opus review confirms the decision record is sufficient

    ## Dependencies

    - MACOS-WBS

    ## Opus review gate

    Mandatory before marking the spike done; if any decision remains below confidence 1.0, create a narrower follow-up spike instead of unblocking implementation.

    ## Out of scope for this request

    Shipping production macOS capture code. This is a spike and decision issue.

    ## Related background

    - Created from tentacle-orchestration planning on 2026-05-22.
    - Opus WBS review verdict: CLEAN with non-blocking advisory notes incorporated where applicable.
    - Target project: https://github.com/users/magicpro97/projects/2

title: QA-01 — ISO 25010 + ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 QA plan and traceability matrix
body:
## Summary

    Create an international-standard QA baseline for the roadmap using ISO/IEC 25010 and ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119.

    ## Why is this needed?

    The user requested QA according to international standards, not only ad hoc tests. Requirements, tests, evidence, and release gates must be traceable.

    ## Context

    The repo already has `docs/04-verification-plan.md` with L1-L5 verification. This issue turns that into a 29119-style test plan and 25010 quality matrix for the macOS/cross-platform roadmap.

    ## Inputs

    - `docs/04-verification-plan.md`
- requirements/design docs
- issue backlog
- ISO/IEC 25010 quality characteristics
- ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 test documentation structure

    ## Outputs

    - Master Test Plan deliverable
- Traceability matrix
- Test-ID naming convention
- Quality threshold table

    ## Test cases

    - [ ] Every P0 requirement maps to at least one test or evidence item
- [ ] Every WBS implementation issue references at least one test ID
- [ ] CI/doc lint fails for missing referenced test IDs once tooling exists
- [ ] Opus QA review validates standards mapping

    ## Acceptance criteria

    - [ ] QA plan covers functional suitability, reliability, performance efficiency, compatibility, security, maintainability, portability, and interaction capability
- [ ] Traceability matrix is complete for P0 scope
- [ ] Opus review confirms standards alignment

    ## Dependencies

    - MACOS-WBS

    ## Opus review gate

    Mandatory; reviewer must be QA-focused and verify standards are not name-dropped without deliverables.

    ## Out of scope for this request

    Purchasing or reproducing copyrighted standards text. Use compliant summaries and project-local templates.

    ## Related background

    - Created from tentacle-orchestration planning on 2026-05-22.
    - Opus WBS review verdict: CLEAN with non-blocking advisory notes incorporated where applicable.
    - Target project: https://github.com/users/magicpro97/projects/2

title: TEST-01 — Deterministic simulation harness for audio, provider, network, PTY, and virtual mic
body:
## Summary

    Design and implement deterministic simulation layers so macOS/live-audio work can be tested without relying only on live meetings.

    ## Why is this needed?

    The roadmap needs clear test and simulation methods for audio, provider failures, terminal behavior, virtual mic routing, and long-running reliability.

    ## Context

    The repo already has fixtures, PTY tests, VMIC evidence, and soak binaries. This issue unifies them into a reproducible simulation strategy for the new roadmap.

    ## Inputs

    - `tests/fixtures/`
- `run_soak`
- `vbcable_ci_probe`
- PTY harness
- contract mocks
- QA-01 test IDs

    ## Outputs

    - Simulation harness plan
- Fixture replayer tasks
- Fake provider/network chaos tasks
- Virtual mic memory-tier tests
- Evidence schema

    ## Test cases

    - [ ] Audio fixture replayer drives pipeline headlessly
- [ ] Provider mock returns 429/503/slow responses and verifies retry/status behavior
- [ ] PTY resize and cleanup tests pass on target platforms
- [ ] Virtual mic memory PCM roundtrip validates RMS/drop/latency gates
- [ ] Soak file-source mode emits schema-versioned JSON

    ## Acceptance criteria

    - [ ] Simulation evidence artifacts are generated in a stable schema
- [ ] L1-L4 can run without a live meeting except explicitly marked hardware tests
- [ ] Opus review is clean

    ## Dependencies

    - QA-01

    ## Opus review gate

    Mandatory; reviewer must check that simulation proves the same criteria as production gates and does not fake success.

    ## Out of scope for this request

    Replacing L5 human acceptance. Simulation reduces risk but does not remove real hardware/reviewer gates.

    ## Related background

    - Created from tentacle-orchestration planning on 2026-05-22.
    - Opus WBS review verdict: CLEAN with non-blocking advisory notes incorporated where applicable.
    - Target project: https://github.com/users/magicpro97/projects/2

title: CI-01 — CI matrix expansion for Windows/macOS/features and required gates
body:
## Summary

    Expand CI to cover Windows and macOS target platforms, feature combinations, MSRV/stable policy, and required release gates.

    ## Why is this needed?

    Current CI is Windows-heavy and lacks macOS jobs. macOS support cannot ship without platform and feature matrix evidence.

    ## Context

    `.github/workflows/ci.yml` already contains fmt, clippy, tests, PTY, VMIC, packaging, soak, and hot-config jobs. This issue extends rather than replaces those gates.

    ## Inputs

    - `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Cargo MSRV 1.86
- Cargo features
- existing VMIC/soak jobs
- QA-01 and TEST-01 outputs

    ## Outputs

    - Windows + macos-13 + macos-14 CI matrix
- MSRV/stable/beta policy
- Feature matrix
- Required status check list

    ## Test cases

    - [ ] Tampering `Cargo.lock` causes `--locked` jobs to fail
- [ ] Required feature combinations compile on macOS and Windows
- [ ] Beta toolchain failure is allowed-fail only where documented
- [ ] VMIC hardware-dependent jobs remain skip-safe with explicit evidence

    ## Acceptance criteria

    - [ ] CI run URL shows required contexts green
- [ ] Branch protection can use the new required checks
- [ ] No existing Windows gate is weakened
- [ ] Opus review is clean

    ## Dependencies

    - QA-01
- TEST-01

    ## Opus review gate

    Mandatory after workflow changes; reviewer must check no release gate was accidentally downgraded.

    ## Out of scope for this request

    Signing/notarization and SBOM/provenance implementation; tracked by SEC-01 and REL-01.

    ## Related background

    - Created from tentacle-orchestration planning on 2026-05-22.
    - Opus WBS review verdict: CLEAN with non-blocking advisory notes incorporated where applicable.
    - Target project: https://github.com/users/magicpro97/projects/2

title: LINUX-01 — Spike: Linux capture backend decision
body:
<!-- linux-cross-platform-quality-roadmap:LINUX-01 -->
Created from tentacle-orchestration Linux/cross-platform roadmap planning.

Parent roadmap ledger: `.github/steps/linux-cross-platform-quality-roadmap.md`.

Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context

Linux needs a WASAPI-loopback equivalent for meeting/system audio. PipeWire is the likely tier-1 path, PulseAudio compatibility is fallback, ALSA-only is best-effort, and portal capture may be needed for sandboxed packages.
## Inputs

`src/audio/wasapi_capture.rs`, `src/audio/mod.rs`, PipeWire/PulseAudio/ALSA/xdg-desktop-portal docs, Ubuntu 22.04/24.04, Fedora 40, Debian 12, one PulseAudio-only environment.
## Outputs

ADR deciding backend order, permissions, latency budget, fallback chain, distro support, and package dependencies.
## Test cases

60-second non-silent 16 kHz mono capture on each target; continuity >= 0.98; first-sample latency <= 200 ms; steady-state p95 <= 60 ms; portal permission-denied UX documented; fallback chain tested.
## Acceptance criteria

Confidence for LINUX-02 becomes 1.0 or implementation stays blocked; evidence stored under `verification-evidence/linux-01/`; Opus review CLEAN.
## Dependencies

XPLAT-01.
## Opus review gate

Mandatory; reviewer must verify measured evidence, not docs-only claims.

## Related existing roadmap issues

- #454 real-time volume evidence where applicable
- #455 real-time voice hot-swap evidence where applicable
- #456 single-active-voice invariant evidence where applicable
- #457 local/remote backend contract where applicable
- #459 QA master plan
- #460 deterministic simulation harness
- #461 CI master plan
- #462 security/supply-chain master plan
- #463 release packaging/channel/rollback master plan

## Project

Add to https://github.com/users/magicpro97/projects/2.

title: TEST-02 — Linux deterministic audio simulation fixture
body:
<!-- linux-cross-platform-quality-roadmap:TEST-02 -->
Created from tentacle-orchestration Linux/cross-platform roadmap planning.

Parent roadmap ledger: `.github/steps/linux-cross-platform-quality-roadmap.md`.

Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context

International QA and CI need hardware-free Linux audio tests for capture, playback, and virtual mic.
## Inputs

#460, PipeWire/PulseAudio null-sink, `pw-cat`, `pactl`, `WavFileSource`, PTY tests, soak runner.

1 kHz tone roundtrip peak in [995, 1005] Hz; silence floor RMS < -60 dBFS; daemon restart recovery; 10 runs produce byte-identical evidence except timestamps; fixture completes <= 90 seconds; zero flakes over 100 runs.
## Acceptance criteria

title: SUPERTONIC-01: Spike feasibility, vendor evidence, and integration shape
body:
## Context
Vendor evidence shows Supertonic is promising, but Windows performance, Rust shutdown behavior, streaming/first-audio latency, and integration shape are unknown.

## Inputs
- Upstream `supertone-inc/supertonic` repo.
- Supertonic 3 Hugging Face model card.
- Rust example and Python `supertonic serve` docs.
- `src/providers/mod.rs`, `src/providers/google/tts.rs`, and local ONNX/ORT patterns from local MT.

## Outputs
Spike report deciding native Rust ONNX vs Python HTTP sidecar vs CLI; rejected alternatives; effort estimate for native provider; list of unresolved blockers.

## Test cases
- Run vendor Rust example offline.
- Run Python local HTTP only as research path.
- Synthesize one `en`, one `ja`, and one `vi` utterance.
- Measure cold start, warm synthesis, time-to-first-audio proxy, RTF, RSS, and whether shutdown is clean without `_exit`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Decision confidence reaches 1.0 for production integration shape.
- Python sidecar is accepted only for research or explicitly rejected for shipping.
- No `src/providers/` implementation starts until this closes.

## Dependencies
SUPERTONIC-WBS.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory; reviewer checks source citations and that no STT/MT scope leaked in.

Parent: #485

title: QA8-01 - QA charter, standards mapping, and risk register
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-01
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
The roadmap needs a single international-standard QA artifact instead of ad hoc stability checks.

## Inputs
#459, #476, #483, ISO/IEC 25010/25022/25023, ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119, IEEE 1633, OWASP ASVS L1, SLSA policy issues.

## Outputs
QA charter, standards compliance matrix, risk register, and traceability template mapping requirement -> ISO characteristic -> risk -> SLO -> test case -> evidence artifact.

## Test cases
Every ISO 25010 characteristic has at least one SLO; every High/Critical risk has at least one Tier-2 or higher test; every 8h stability SLO maps to an evidence artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
Matrix covers Windows/macOS/Linux; doc/schema checks pass; #459/#476 are linked as parent QA references; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-WBS.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-02 - Machine-readable SLO schema and gate checker
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-02
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
Release gates must be machine-verifiable rather than prose-only.

## Inputs
Accepted SLO draft, current metrics snapshot fields, `frame_pacing_bench` JSON mode, soak reports.

## Outputs
Versioned SLO schema, pass/fail gate checker, synthetic pass/fail fixtures, and CI integration plan.

## Test cases
Synthetic clean run passes; one fixture fails each category (crash, frame, RSS slope, CPU, queue, audio, provider, virtual mic); malformed evidence fails with a clear message.

## Acceptance criteria
Checker exits non-zero for any blocker gate failure; it is reusable by `nfr-verification-gate`; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-01.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-03 - Soak evidence schema v2 and telemetry export contract
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-03
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
Existing soak artifacts are not rich enough to prove 8h stability, 60fps, no crash, and cross-platform parity.

## Inputs
`tests/soak/run_soak.rs`, `src/metrics/snapshot.rs`, `src/metrics/process.rs`, `src/tui/frame_pacer.rs`, current `audio_stability_proof` JSON.

## Outputs
Additive schema v2 with run metadata, host/build/config hash, samples, frame pacing, audio capture, provider, virtual mic, Tokio, crash, fault-injection, RSS slope, handles/FDs, and attachments.

## Test cases
v1 fields remain readable; v2 golden sample validates; a 90-second dry-run produces at least three timestamped samples; secret-stripped config hash is deterministic.

## Acceptance criteria
Schema is documented and consumed by QA8-02; no breaking removal of existing report fields; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-02.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-04 - Cross-platform process, memory, handle, FD, and thread probes
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-04
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
Current process metrics need cross-platform parity and leak signals for 8-hour runs.

## Inputs
`src/metrics/process.rs`, `src/metrics/memory_guard.rs`, Windows psapi, macOS `task_info`/`proc_pidinfo`, Linux `/proc/self/status` and `/proc/self/fd`.

## Outputs
Platform probe trait, Windows/macOS/Linux implementations, thread/handle/FD counts, private bytes/physical footprint where supported, and overhead notes.

## Test cases
Values are non-zero on each OS; injected fixture leak is detected; sampling overhead <= 0.5% CPU; unavailable metrics are explicit `unsupported`, not zero-shaped success.

## Acceptance criteria
8h schema can report RSS, private bytes, thread count, and handle/FD count on all three platforms; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-03.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-05 - 8-hour soak runner v2 with fault injection
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-05
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
Existing CI validates fixtures and short dry-runs, but the user needs an 8-hour crash-free meeting proof.

## Inputs
QA8-02/03/04, existing soak runner, `tests/soak/soak_audio.wav`, #495 lower-tier soak, mock provider scripts from #460.

## Outputs
`--hours 8`, `--sample-secs 30`, `--fault-script`, `--crash-watch`, schema v2 artifact output, and fault-event recording.

## Test cases
10-minute smoke produces a complete v2 artifact; 1-hour deterministic schedule records network outage/provider 429/device hot-swap/CPU pressure events; forced panic creates actionable crash evidence.

## Acceptance criteria
One 8-hour green run per platform can be recorded for a single `git_sha`; all QA8-02 blocker gates pass; `tui-soak-monitor` review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-03, QA8-04, QA8-09.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-07 - Audio capture, provider, and virtual-mic backpressure telemetry
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-07
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
8h stability can fail through audio jitter, provider queue growth, stale cancellation, fanout drops, or virtual-mic underruns without crashing.

## Inputs
`src/audio/wasapi_capture.rs`, future macOS/Linux capture backends, `src/audio/fanout.rs`, `src/pipeline/audio_sink.rs`, `src/pipeline/mod.rs`, #495, and prior audio/provider/virtual-mic issues linked by search at implementation time.

## Outputs
Audio inter-chunk jitter histogram, capture stall counters, provider queue/inflight/error recovery counters, cancellation latency histogram, virtual-mic latency and underrun telemetry.

## Test cases
Replayer with injected gap increments capture stall; synthetic provider outage records recovery; synthetic sink underrun is counted; no fanout drops under nominal dual mode.

## Acceptance criteria
QA8-05 artifacts report all telemetry sections and enforce audio/provider/virtual-mic thresholds; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-03, QA8-06.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-08 - Panic, OOM, dump capture, and symbolication workflow
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-08
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
"No crash" needs platform-native evidence and an actionable root-cause workflow if a crash occurs.

## Inputs
`src/main.rs`, Tokio task supervision, `crash-root-cause` agent, Windows WER LocalDumps, macOS DiagnosticReports, Linux `systemd-coredump`/core files, release debug symbols.

## Outputs
Panic hook, metrics flush on panic, crash-watch docs/scripts, `.pdb`/`.dSYM`/`.debug` collection plan, symbolication commands, normalized crash JSON node.

## Test cases
Forced panic writes `panic-log.txt`; Windows/macOS/Linux crash watchers collect a sentinel crash artifact; symbolication produces a backtrace text artifact; dump scrubber removes secrets before upload.

## Acceptance criteria
QA8-05 cannot pass if any dump/crash artifact exists; `crash-root-cause` review CLEAN for failure workflow.

## Dependencies
QA8-03, QA8-04.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-11 - Project priority and issue hygiene enforcement
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-11
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
The user requires every new issue to be P0 in Project #2 and no new issue should miss priority.

## Inputs
Project #2 ID `PVT_kwHOAT2vsM4BXVxn`, Priority field `PVTSSF_lAHOAT2vsM4BXVxnzhSjUPQ`, P0 option `b654f0a5`, label `priority:P0`, issue/project GraphQL APIs.

## Outputs
Issue hygiene workflow or script that applies/validates exactly one `priority:*` label and syncs Project #2 `Priority` field; one-time audit/backfill report for this WBS issue set.

## Test cases
Issue without priority gets `priority:P0`; Project item Priority is set to P0; mismatch is reported; created QA8 issues audit returns `missing_label=0` and `missing_project_priority=0`.

## Acceptance criteria
All QA8 issues have label `priority:P0` and Project #2 Priority P0; weekly drift audit plan exists; Opus review CLEAN.

## Dependencies
QA8-WBS.

## Opus review gate
Mandatory.

Parent: #498

title: QA8-12 - Release gate orchestrator and Opus/NFR review workflow
body:
Roadmap marker: eight-hour-stability-qa-roadmap:QA8-12
Created from tentacle-orchestration 8-hour stability QA roadmap planning. Final Opus WBS review: CLEAN, confidence 1.0.

## Context
Each completed work package must be reviewed, and release promotion must consume the evidence instead of relying on claims.

No QA8 child can be closed without evidence links and Opus/specialist review result; RC/GA gates consume the same evidence bundle; Opus review CLEAN.
